Line_References: Two Python scripts with different purposes: pytest_pyspark_tests provides comprehensive unit testing for PySpark data transformations, while ABAP_to_PySpark_Conversion_Tester implements an ETL validation pipeline. Both contain syntax errors that impact correctness scores.

Details: Scripts serve different purposes - one for unit testing PySpark transformations (pytest_pyspark_tests) and one for ABAP-to-PySpark conversion validation (ABAP_to_PySpark_Conversion_Tester). Both involve data validation concepts but with fundamentally different approaches and objectives.

Completely different architectural patterns: pytest_pyspark_tests uses test-driven structure with fixtures and test functions, while ABAP_to_PySpark_Conversion_Tester follows sequential ETL pipeline pattern with step-by-step functions. Minimal structural overlap.
Generally well-formed pytest structure but contains syntax issues with exception handling in test_TC005_InvalidDataType function. Missing specific exception types and some logical flow issues.

Multiple syntax errors including invalid variable names 'ABAP_to_PySpark_Conversion_Tester.csv' (contains dots), undefined variables, and malformed string literals 'ELANSURIYAA/AAVA_Testingrt' throughout the code.
Average correctness score reflecting syntax issues in both outputs, with pytest_pyspark_tests being more syntactically sound than ABAP_to_PySpark_Conversion_Tester.
